name: zsnd_play_handle_stop_if_active
description: zSndPlayHandle::StopIfActive stops or delegates an active sound handle on the selected backend.
known_limits: VC5SP3 11.00.7022 /O2 /Ob1 /GX /Zp4 COFF byte compare fails with 135 unmasked mismatches after relocation masking; Binary Ninja body is 275 bytes and the VC object body is 288 bytes with 15 trailing VC NOPs trimmed.

Current profile sweep found every tested VC5SP3 and VC6 profile tied at 135 mismatches with the same 275-byte BN body, 288-byte VC object body, 60 relocation bytes masked, and 15 trailing VC NOPs trimmed.

Reviewed drift is concentrated in backend dispatch and block layout: the current explicit backend branch improves the old switch shape but still lays out DirectSound before the unsupported-backend/A3D tail, while retail branches forward to the DirectSound block and falls through A3D before the default return.

Rejected source-shape attempt: spelling the backend dispatch as a case-1-first switch preserves functional behavior and emits the desired sub/dec dispatch, but places the default return before the A3D body and worsens the VC5SP3 byte compare to 152 mismatches with 12 trailing VC NOPs trimmed.

Rejected source-shape attempt: spelling the backend dispatch as a case-0-first switch also worsens the VC5SP3 byte compare to 152 mismatches with 12 trailing VC NOPs trimmed; an explicit goto-shaped dispatch optimizes back to the current 135-mismatch baseline.

The native smoke covers the initialization guard, unsupported-backend default-return path without asserting the unstable uninitialized value, stream request delegation, DirectSound GetStatus/Restore/Stop path with last-voice clearing, A3D Stop path, and missing backend failure.
